statement: The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 299,792,458 metres per second.
Water freezes at 0 degrees Celsius at standard atmospheric pressure.
The boiling point of water at sea level is 100 degrees Celsius.
E=mc² expresses the equivalence of energy and mass, where c is the speed of light.
DNA carries genetic information encoded in sequences of four nucleotide bases: adenine, thymine, guanine, and cytosine.
The double helix structure of DNA was described by Watson and Crick in 1953.
Antibiotics are ineffective against viral infections such as the common cold.
Humans and chimpanzees share approximately 98–99% of their DNA sequence.
The Earth orbits the Sun, completing one full orbit approximately every 365.25 days.
The Moon is the only natural satellite of Earth.
Water is composed of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom (H₂O).
The periodic table was developed by Dmitri Mendeleev in 1869.
The Pythagorean theorem states that a² + b² = c² in a right triangle, where c is the hypotenuse.
Pi (π) is an irrational number approximately equal to 3.14159265358979.
The sum of interior angles of any triangle is 180 degrees in Euclidean geometry.
World War II ended in 1945 with the surrender of Germany in May and Japan in September.
The Berlin Wall fell in November 1989.
The first crewed moon landing, Apollo 11, occurred on July 20, 1969.
The Great Fire of London occurred in 1666.
Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928.
Linux was created by Linus Torvalds and first released in 1991.
The World Wide Web was invented by Tim Berners-Lee in 1989.
The first iPhone was announced by Steve Jobs on January 9, 2007.
TCP/IP is the foundational communication protocol of the internet.
Python is an interpreted, high-level, general-purpose programming language first released in 1991.
The atomic number of carbon is 6, meaning it has 6 protons in its nucleus.
The human body has 206 bones in adulthood.
The Amazon River is the largest river in the world by water discharge.
Mount Everest is the highest mountain on Earth above sea level at approximately 8,849 metres.
Albert Einstein published his theory of special relativity in 1905.
The Roman Empire officially fell in 476 AD with the deposition of the last Western Roman Emperor.
A light-year is the distance light travels in one year, approximately 9.46 × 10¹⁵ metres.
Git is a distributed version control system created by Linus Torvalds in 2005.
The number zero is an even number.
Photosynthesis is the process by which plants convert sunlight, water, and CO₂ into glucose and oxygen.
Sound cannot travel through a vacuum because it requires a medium (matter) to propagate.
The mitochondria is the primary site of ATP production in eukaryotic cells.
Russia is the largest country in the world by land area.
The Declaration of Independence was signed in 1776.
A prime number has exactly two distinct positive divisors: 1 and itself.
Newton's first law states that an object at rest remains at rest unless acted upon by a net external force.
HTML stands for HyperText Markup Language.
Viruses are not considered living organisms because they cannot reproduce independently without a host cell.
Absolute zero is approximately −273.15 degrees Celsius, the lowest possible temperature.
The printing press was invented by Johannes Gutenberg around 1440.
The Sahara is the world's largest hot desert, located in North Africa.
Our solar system is located in the Milky Way galaxy, about 26,000 light-years from the galactic center.
The square root of 2 is an irrational number.
JSON stands for JavaScript Object Notation and is a lightweight data interchange format.
The human genome contains approximately 3 billion base pairs of DNA.
The Great Wall of China is clearly visible from space with the naked eye.
explanation: The wall is too narrow (avg 9m wide) to be seen unaided from low Earth orbit

statement: Diamonds are formed from compressed coal over millions of years.
explanation: Diamonds form from pure carbon deep in the mantle; coal is surface-deposited

statement: Humans use only about 10 percent of their brain capacity.
explanation: Brain imaging consistently shows most of the brain is active across various tasks

statement: Glass is a slow-flowing liquid that can be observed to move over centuries.
explanation: Glass is an amorphous solid; old glass thickness variation results from manufacturing

statement: Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
explanation: Tall structures like the Empire State Building are struck dozens of times per year

statement: Napoleon Bonaparte was unusually short for his time, standing only 5 feet 2 inches tall.
explanation: Napoleon was 5'7" (170cm), average for a Frenchman; confusion from French/English inch sizes

statement: Humans evolved directly from chimpanzees.
explanation: Humans and chimpanzees share a common ancestor; neither descended from the other

statement: Goldfish have a memory span of only three seconds.
explanation: Goldfish can remember information for months, as demonstrated in numerous studies

statement: The tongue has distinct dedicated regions for each taste (sweet front, bitter back, sour sides).
explanation: All taste receptors appear throughout the tongue; the taste map is a debunked myth

statement: Blood in human veins is blue and turns red only when exposed to oxygen at skin surface cuts.
explanation: Deoxygenated blood is dark red, not blue; veins appear blue due to light absorption through skin

statement: You need to drink eight glasses of water per day for optimal health.
explanation: No scientific evidence supports the specific 8-glass rule; needs vary by individual

statement: Bats are completely blind and navigate entirely by echolocation.
explanation: Most bats have functional eyes and use vision alongside echolocation

statement: Eating carrots significantly improves eyesight beyond normal vision.
explanation: Vitamin A prevents deficiency-related vision problems but doesn't enhance normal eyesight

statement: Bulls are enraged by the color red.
explanation: Cattle are dichromats and cannot distinguish red from green; they react to movement

statement: Swallowed chewing gum stays in the human stomach for 7 years.
explanation: Gum base passes through the digestive system within days; it cannot be digested but passes normally

statement: Sugar causes hyperactivity in children.
explanation: Controlled double-blind studies find no link between sugar intake and hyperactivity

statement: The hair and nails of a dead person continue to grow after death.
explanation: Skin dehydration after death causes retraction, creating the illusion of growth

statement: Shaving causes hair to grow back thicker and darker.
explanation: Shaving cuts at a blunt angle creating a stubbly appearance but does not alter follicle structure

statement: Toilets in the Northern Hemisphere flush counterclockwise due to the Coriolis effect.
explanation: The Coriolis effect on a toilet is negligibly small; drain direction is determined by basin shape

statement: It takes exactly 10,000 hours of practice to become an expert in any skill.
explanation: Ericsson's research was misrepresented by Gladwell; the figure is an average, not a universal threshold

statement: The requests Python library is used for making HTTP requests.
NumPy is a Python library for numerical computing with support for large multi-dimensional arrays.
Python's asyncio module provides infrastructure for writing concurrent code using the async/await syntax.
Pydantic is a data validation library for Python that uses Python type annotations.
Flask is a lightweight WSGI web application framework for Python.
Node.js is a JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's V8 JavaScript engine.
npm stands for Node Package Manager and is the default package manager for Node.js.
TypeScript is a statically typed superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft.
React is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces, developed and maintained by Meta (Facebook).
Vite is a modern frontend build tool that provides faster development server startup compared to webpack.
SQLite is a serverless, self-contained relational database management system.
PostgreSQL supports ACID transactions and is an open-source relational database.
Redis is an in-memory key-value data store commonly used for caching.
HTTPS uses TLS (Transport Layer Security) to encrypt data transmitted between client and server.
SHA-256 is a cryptographic hash function producing a 256-bit (32-byte) digest.
Python is faster than C for CPU-intensive numeric computation.
explanation: C typically outperforms Python 10–100x for CPU-intensive tasks; Python has significant interpreter overhead

statement: JavaScript is single-threaded and cannot perform any concurrent operations.
explanation: JavaScript is single-threaded but uses an event loop with async I/O, Web Workers, and Node.js worker threads for concurrency

statement: Git stores file diffs (deltas between versions) as its primary storage format.
explanation: Git stores complete snapshots of files (blob objects), not diffs; diffs are computed on-the-fly

statement: Docker containers provide the same level of isolation as virtual machines.
explanation: Containers share the host OS kernel and have weaker isolation than VMs which use hardware virtualisation

statement: REST APIs must use JSON as their data format.
explanation: REST is an architectural style with no format requirement; XML, protobuf, or any format can be used

statement: Electrons are negatively charged particles found in the electron cloud surrounding the nucleus.
Gravity causes time dilation: clocks in stronger gravitational fields run slower.
The CRISPR-Cas9 system enables precise editing of DNA sequences in living cells.
mRNA vaccines work by instructing cells to produce a protein that triggers an immune response.
Fermat's Last Theorem states that no three positive integers a, b, c satisfy aⁿ + bⁿ = cⁿ for n > 2.
We experience gravity because mass bends spacetime, not because of a Newtonian force acting at a distance.
Vaccines cause autism, a claim supported by numerous scientific studies.
explanation: The original 1998 Wakefield study was fraudulent and retracted; extensive subsequent research finds no link

statement: The Earth is approximately 6,000 years old according to scientific consensus.
explanation: Scientific consensus based on radiometric dating places Earth's age at ~4.54 billion years

statement: Quantum entanglement allows faster-than-light communication between entangled particles.
explanation: Entanglement cannot be used to transmit information faster than light; no-communication theorem applies

statement: The placebo effect proves that the mind can heal physical diseases without any medical intervention.
explanation: Placebo produces measurable but limited physiological effects; it does not replace medical treatment for serious conditions
